Geo / Map

Research Toolchain Overview

1. `tiff.info`

Reads image metadata (path, width/height, bands, etc.) as the starting point for all downstream tools.

2. `image.tile` (On Demand)

Splits large images into tiles when needed; usually skipped for small images to reduce extra computation.

3. `llm.first_pass`

Performs first-pass scene screening and provides semantic evidence without emitting numeric risk scores.

4. `seg.run`

Generates pixel-level segmentation masks and estimates landslide coverage as core cross-check evidence.

5. `boundary.extract` (Boundary Extraction, formerly `seg.refine`)

Reuses the `seg.run` mask to extract candidate regions and build a boundary overlay for later review.

6. `seg.llm_review` (Optional)

When LLM review is enabled, the system calls `seg.llm_review` after `boundary.extract`: instead of patch crops, it sends the full image with boundary overlay for descriptive review, without adding numeric confidence.

7. `cls.run` (ConvNeXt, Optional)

Runs landslide type classification and returns class/confidence/top-k as reference only, not as a yes/no vote.

8. `geo.nearby` (OSM, Optional)

With observation coordinates, queries nearby roads, settlements, buildings, and amenities to enrich spatial context.

9. `geo.background` (Geologic Background, Optional)

Queries geologic context by coordinates, including reverse address, DEM elevation, slope/aspect, and lithology/stratigraphy.

10. `fuse.decision`

Fuses first-pass, classification, boundary evidence, and geo context into the final decision and explanation.

Qwen Live Token Stream
